seo.meta_title: Mount Rinjani 2-Day Senaru Crater Rim Trek — No Summit | Go2Rinjani
seo.meta_description: Hike Mount Rinjani's Senaru crater rim in 2 days — trek through dense rainforest to Pelawangan Senaru at 2,641 m with panoramic views over Segara Anak lake. No summit required. All levels welcome.

hero.title: Mount Rinjani 2-Day Senaru Trek — Crater Rim Through the Rainforest
hero.desc: Not every Rinjani hike has to end at the summit — Go2Rinjani's 2-day Senaru crater rim trek takes you through the dense tropical rainforest of Rinjani National Park to Pelawangan Senaru at 2,641 m, with sweeping panoramic views over Segara Anak lake and Mount Rinjani's massive caldera. A spectacular Rinjani experience, accessible to all fitness levels.

intro.label: 2 Days 1 Night — Senaru Route
intro.title: The Most Accessible Rinjani Hike — Spectacular Views, No Summit Required
intro.desc: The 2-day Senaru crater rim trek is the ideal entry point into Mount Rinjani National Park for trekkers who want a genuine mountain experience without the physical demands of a summit push. Starting from the ancient Sasak village of Senaru on the western slopes of Rinjani, the trail winds through dense tropical rainforest rich in biodiversity before arriving at Pelawangan Senaru — the Senaru crater rim at 2,641 m — with unobstructed views over Segara Anak lake and Rinjani's volcanic caldera. Go2Rinjani's certified local guides lead every step of the way.

highlights.section_title: What to Expect on This Trek

items.title: Senaru Village Trailhead
items.desc: The trek begins at the ancient Sasak village of Senaru on the western slopes of Mount Rinjani National Park — a culturally rich starting point where local traditions and mountain life intersect before the rainforest trail begins.

items.title: Dense Tropical Rainforest
items.desc: The Senaru route is defined by its lush, dense tropical rainforest — a completely different landscape from the open savannahs of Sembalun. Trekkers pass through towering trees, rich biodiversity, and the sounds of Rinjani's untouched natural environment.

items.title: Pelawangan Senaru Crater Rim at 2,641 m
items.desc: Reach Pelawangan Senaru — the Senaru crater rim at 2,641 m — with panoramic views over Segara Anak lake, the turquoise sacred crater lake of Mount Rinjani, and the summit rising to 3,726 m on the opposite side of the caldera.

items.title: Panoramic Views Over Rinjani's Caldera
items.desc: From Pelawangan Senaru, the full scale of Mount Rinjani's volcanic caldera is revealed — Segara Anak lake below, the summit at 3,726 m across, and on clear mornings, Mount Agung in Bali visible across the Lombok Strait.

itinerary.intro: Two days, one night on the Senaru route — rainforest, crater rim, and panoramic views over Segara Anak lake without the summit push.

days.title: Senaru Village to Pelawangan Senaru Crater Rim
days.desc: After breakfast and pre-trek briefing at your accommodation in Senaru, Go2Rinjani transfers you to the Senaru trailhead at the edge of the village. Trek through dense tropical rainforest and three rest posts before arriving at Pelawangan Senaru crater rim (2,641 m) by late afternoon. Dinner and overnight camping at the crater rim with views over Segara Anak lake and Rinjani's caldera.
highlights: Transfer to Senaru trailhead at village edge
Trek through dense tropical rainforest — Post 1, 2, 3
Arrive Pelawangan Senaru crater rim (2,641 m)
Panoramic views over Segara Anak lake & Rinjani caldera
Dinner & overnight camp at Pelawangan Senaru

days.title: Sunrise at Pelawangan Senaru — Descent to Senaru Village
days.desc: Wake before dawn for sunrise over Mount Rinjani's caldera and Segara Anak lake from Pelawangan Senaru. After breakfast at camp, begin the descent via the same rainforest trail back to Senaru village. Transfer back to accommodation after completing the trek.
highlights: Pre-dawn sunrise over Rinjani caldera & Segara Anak lake
Breakfast at Pelawangan Senaru crater rim camp

inclusions.intro: Every 2-day Mount Rinjani Senaru crater rim trek with Go2Rinjani is fully supported — here is exactly what is included.
included: Porter (personal camp equipment up to 25 kg)
Mount Rinjani National Park entrance permit
All meals on the mountain (3x daily) + hot drinks + mineral water
Full camping equipment: tent, sleeping bag, mattress, pillow, cooking gear
Hotel pickup and drop-off (Senaru, Mataram, Airport, Senggigi, Bangsal)
excluded: Personal trekking gear (jacket, trekking shoes, trekking poles, daypack)
Tips for guides and porters (appreciated but not mandatory)
